misconceptionSummary: Students think a negative exponent makes the result negative.

visuals.ruleText: A negative exponent means take the reciprocal — flip the power to the denominator

steps.narration: A negative exponent does not make the result negative. It means divide one by the positive version. Ten to the minus three is one divided by ten to the three — one divided by one thousand.
Each negative power of ten is a unit fraction — one over a power of ten. Ten to the minus two is one hundredth; ten to the minus three is one thousandth.

lines.text: Negative powers name the decimal columns

steps.narration: Every decimal column in the place value system is a negative power of ten. Tenths is ten to the minus one, hundredths is ten to the minus two. Negative powers and decimal place value are the same idea.
Ten to the minus two equals one hundredth equals zero point zero one. Three ways to say the same thing — a small positive fraction, never a negative number.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: thinking 10⁻² = −100. The negative exponent means reciprocal, not negative value. 10⁻² = 1/100 = 0.01 — a small positive number.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Negative exponent does not mean negative result. It means 1 divided by the positive power. 10⁻² is one hundredth, which is positive and very small.
